perturbation.original_text: 11. 7. 1 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this AGREEMENT and in particular, clause 11. 1, in instances where the module of the SOFTWARE constituting a portion of the Marketplace known as MarketSet 2. 0 Platform, fails and cannot be accessed or USED by TELKOM and BUSINESS THIRD PARTIES on the DESIGNATED UNIT and/or such other DESIGNATED UNIT where the SOFTWARE is installed for disaster recovery or back-up USE, and such failure is caused solely by a failure in the SOFTWARE to perform in accordance with the specifications set out in the DOCUMENTATION and should the Marketplace remain unable to be accessed or USED by TELKOM and/or BUSINESS THIRD PARTIES for a continuous period of 72 hours, commencing from the time when such failure is logged as a Priority 1 error (as defined in annexure 2) with SAP AFRICA as a SOFTWARE problem and SAP AFRICA is unable to provide a solution or resolution within the said 72 hours, then TELKOM shall be entitled to terminate this AGREEMENT and claim damages limited as provided for in clause 11. 4.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. 7. 1 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this AGREEMENT and in particular, clause 11. 1, in instances where the module of the SOFTWARE constituting a portion of the Marketplace known as MarketSet 2. 0 Platform, fails and cannot be accessed or USED by TELKOM and BUSINESS THIRD PARTIES on the DESIGNATED UNIT and/or such other DESIGNATED UNIT where the SOFTWARE is installed for disaster recovery or back-up USE, and such failure is caused solely by a failure in the SOFTWARE to perform in accordance with the specifications set out in the DOCUMENTATION and should the Marketplace remain unable to be accessed or USED by TELKOM and/or BUSINESS THIRD PARTIES for a continuous period of 72 hours, commencing from the time when such failure is logged as a Priority 1 error (as defined in annexure 2) with SAP AFRICA as a SOFTWARE problem and SAP AFRICA is unable to provide a solution or resolution within the said 24 hours, then TELKOM shall be entitled to terminate this AGREEMENT and claim damages limited as provided for in clause 11. 4.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by modifying the time frame, that TELKOM is unable to use the SOFTWARE, to claim damages and be entitled to terminate the AGREEMENT from 72 hours to 24 hours. This contradiction may cause, a conflicting interpertation of the contract terms, since one can say it's 72 hours and other can say is 24 hours. Furthermore, it is uncertain to determine which term should be enforced
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. 7. 1 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this AGREEMENT and in particular, clause 11. 1, in instances where the module of the SOFTWARE constituting a portion of the Marketplace known as MarketSet 2. 0 Platform, fails and cannot be accessed or USED by TELKOM and BUSINESS THIRD PARTIES on the DESIGNATED UNIT and/or such other DESIGNATED UNIT where the SOFTWARE is installed for disaster recovery or back-up USE, and such failure is caused solely by a failure in the SOFTWARE to perform in accordance with the specifications set out in the DOCUMENTATION and should the Marketplace remain unable to be accessed or USED by TELKOM and/or BUSINESS THIRD PARTIES for a continuous period of 72 hours, commencing from the time when such failure is logged as a Priority 1 error (as defined in annexure 2) with SAP AFRICA as a SOFTWARE problem and SAP AFRICA is unable to provide a solution or resolution within the said 24 hours, then TELKOM shall be entitled to terminate this AGREEMENT and claim damages limited as provided for in clause 11. 4.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the timeframe for TELKOM's entitlement to terminate the agreement and claim damages from 72 hours to 24 hours. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, this creates a clear inconsistency within the document, as the same clause now stipulates two different timeframes (72 hours and 24 hours) for the same event. This directly contradicts the original agreement, making it unclear which term should be enforced, leading to potential disputes and impacting the enforceability of the termination clause as defined in Perturbation Definitions point 5 (Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction).
